gamma.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_oeis invocation=tool/oeis_live_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)

core_5_op_kernel.1_intake_topic: Evaluate whether /Users/ghost/core/hive/packages/ai/src/utils/event-stream.ts should be ported into the coding-agent core HTTP SSE transport.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: topology-match
2_pre_register_falsifiers.predicate: EventStream can replace or directly underwrite SSE fan-out without per-client duplication or dropped events.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: terminal-mismatch
2_pre_register_falsifiers.predicate: EventStream completion contract aligns with open-ended SSE lifetime.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: blast-radius-bounded
2_pre_register_falsifiers.predicate: Any useful unification remains small enough to justify the churn.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: payload-shape-overlap
2_pre_register_falsifiers.predicate: AssistantMessageEvent and ServerEvent overlap enough to share a primitive without forcing lossy abstraction.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: alternative-already-fits
2_pre_register_falsifiers.predicate: No existing primitive already solves SSE fan-out cleanly.

2_pre_register_falsifiers.name: test-surface-stable
2_pre_register_falsifiers.predicate: Porting/unifying would not destabilize provider-tier result() and current SSE parsing tests.

3_fan_out_axes.rationale: terminal final-result promise vs long-lived heartbeat transport
surface area touched by any real unification
LLM response delta protocol vs server observability envelope
existing bus fit plus provider/test contract stability

core_5_op_kernel.4_gate_tier1: Promote only if a stable falsifier-pass pattern supports a design conclusion.

source_primitive.shape: EventStream<T,R> async iterable with queue, waiting waiters, done flag, finalResultPromise, plus AssistantMessageEventStream specialization

observed_behavior.fanout: push() resolves at most one waiter via waiting.shift(); queued events are drained by one iterator
observed_behavior.completion: result() resolves once done or error event arrives

target_transport.shape: Node HTTP SSE adapter over bus.subscribeAll with optional session filter and heartbeat

observed_behavior.fanout: one subscribeAll consumer per connected client
observed_behavior.lifetime: connection remains open until disconnect; no terminal message contract required

existing_fit_primitive.shape: typed EventEmitter bus with publish, subscribe, subscribeAll, listenerCount, clear
existing_fit_primitive.fit_reason: already matches multi-consumer SSE fan-out directly

falsifier_outcomes.evidence: Topology mismatch is real. EventStream wakes one waiter and drains one shared queue; SSE currently subscribes each client independently through bus.subscribeAll.
Completion mismatch is real. EventStream exposes finalResultPromise/result(); SSE deliberately stays open with heartbeat until socket close.

Broad unification reaches 13 provider files that directly touch AssistantMessageEventStream plus ai/src/api-registry.ts, ai/src/types.ts, ai/src/stream.ts, coding-agent/src/core/model-registry.ts, and coding-agent/src/core/extensions/types.ts. Narrow reuse inside sse.ts is small but yields no new capability.
Payload families are different. ServerEventTopic has 19 observability topics; AssistantMessageEvent is the provider/token/tool-delta protocol used to assemble a final AssistantMessage.
The alternative already fits: bus.subscribeAll is exactly what sse.ts needs for per-client broadcast.
ai/src/stream.ts complete() and completeSimple() still depend on s.result(), and ai/test/anthropic-sse-parsing.test.ts still exercises provider-side SSE parsing independently of server SSE transport.

axes_implemented.decision: Port is refuted. EventStream is not a broadcast primitive.
Port is refuted. EventStream is terminal-result-centric; SSE is disconnect-centric.
Broad unification is too expensive for the payoff; narrow unification is cheap but pointless.
Port is refuted. The envelopes represent different protocol families and lifecycle assumptions.
Port is refuted. Existing bus infrastructure already fits, and provider-side result()/test surfaces would inherit unnecessary risk.

tier_1_promotions.claim: Do not port event-stream.ts into core HTTP SSE. Keep EventStream for provider-side single-consumer terminal completion, and keep bus.ts plus sse.ts for multi-consumer open-ended transport.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: F100 PASS, F101 PASS, F103 PASS, F104 PASS, F105 PASS; F102 only passes under the no-payoff narrow scope.

tier_1_promotions.claim: If a future non-HTTP consumer genuinely needs for-await semantics over bus events, add a local adapter near bus.ts instead of reusing EventStream.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: Preserves bus broadcast topology and avoids coupling SSE to result().

roi_verdict.summary: The port loses on topology, completion semantics, payload overlap, and alternatives. The only low-churn version adds abstraction but no benefit; the useful versions spread churn into the provider stack.

honesty_triad.write_barrier: No source edits beyond this witness artifact.
no_fabrication_evidence: Read event-stream.ts end-to-end and verified queue, waiting, done, result(), and AssistantMessageEventStream semantics.
Read coding-agent server sse.ts end-to-end and verified per-client bus.subscribeAll fan-out, session filter, heartbeat, and disconnect cleanup.
Read coding-agent server bus.ts end-to-end and verified typed publish/subscribeAll already exists.
Read server types.ts and verified ServerEventTopic currently enumerates 19 topics including heartbeat.
Read ai/src/stream.ts and verified complete()/completeSimple() still depend on s.result().
Re-counted current provider touch files and contract surfaces from the live tree.

next_cycle_handoff.reopen_only_if: a concrete consumer needs AsyncIterable semantics over bus events
next_cycle_handoff.recommended_action: add a tiny bus-to-AsyncIterable adapter local to coding-agent transport/core rather than porting EventStream
